pages.raw_text:
Mr.. j. F. Collins, manager of the
Spokane & Montana Gold Mining q
Milling company, corporation co,h-
posed of Spokane men, arrived from the
camp a few days ago and reports work
progressing favorably on the proper
ties of the company in the Cabinet
mountains, West Fisher district, south
of the famous Snowshoe Mine, in Mon-
tana A new fourfoot ledge has been
struck on the Keystone. of the group,
carrying free milling $25 ore, indie
pendent of other lodges already open
ed up, also making a very fine showing
with flakes of gold visible to the eye.
assaying up in the hundreds. Mr.. Col
Inns brought with him some very fine
specimens of, the ore just discovered.
which can be seen at the company $
office. No. O4l7 Monroe street. He
has taken contract for $50 feet of
tunneling which. when completed, it
is expected will justify the company
installing machinery and stamp mill
and ought to place the property in
the list of regular producers of the
camp. He is taking force with him
to prosecute development. which will
be augmented as fast as required.
